description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked, dark wooden logs, giving it a robust and traditional appearance. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with wooden shingles, extending slightly beyond the walls to provide some overhang. The cabin features small, rectangular windows with white frames, symmetrically placed on the longer sides and at the gable ends. There are two visible doors, both made of dark wood, one of which is located near the center of the longer facade. The structure sits directly on the ground without any visible foundation or porch, emphasizing its simple, utilitarian design. The overall look is reminiscent of early American or Scandinavian log cabins, suitable for rural or forested urban park settings.
description: A traditional log cabin with a steep shingled roof, dark wood walls, and small, white-framed windows. The design is simple and rustic, with two visible doors and no porch.
description_view_0: The long side of the cabin is visible, showing two doors and three windows, with the shingled roof dominating the view.
description_view_1: The gable end is shown, featuring two small, symmetrically placed windows and the log construction detail.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is visible, displaying three windows and no doors, with the same shingled roof overhead.
description_view_3: The other gable end is shown, mirroring the first with two small windows and log construction.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with wooden shingles and moderate overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple small, rectangular windows symmetrically distributed: three on each long side and two on each gable end, all with white frames.
attribute_door_placement: Two exterior doors, both on the same long side, one near the center and one offset toward the left.
